author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 48–59.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that lesson of the holy Gospel which has been read to-day, from power we learn patience.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what are we as servants to the Lord, as sinners to the Just One, as creatures to the Creator?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Howbeit, just as in what we are evil, we are so of ourselves; so in whatever respects we are good, we are so of Him, and through Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And nothing does man so seek as he does power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has great power in the Lord Christ; but let him first imitate His patience, that he may attain to power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who of us would listen with patience if it were said to him, “Thou hast a devil”? as was said to Him, who was not only bringing men to salvation, but also subjecting devils to His authority.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Jews had said, “Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?” of these two charges cast at Him, He denied the one, but not the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He answered and said, “I have not a devil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, I am not a Samaritan; and yet the two charges had been made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although He returned not cursing with cursing, although He met not slander with slander, yet was it proper for Him to deny the one charge and not to deny the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And not without a purpose, brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Samaritan means keeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew that He was our keeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “He that keepeth Israel neither slumbereth nor sleepeth;” and, “Except the Lord keep the city, they wake in vain who keep it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then is our Keeper who is our Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For did it belong to Him to redeem us, and would it not be His to preserve us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, that you may know more fully the hidden reason why He ought not to have denied that He was a Samaritan, call to mind that well-known parable, where a certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, who wounded him severely, and left him half dead on the road.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A priest came along and took no notice of him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A Levite came up, and he also passed on his way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain Samaritan came up—He who is our Keeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He went up to the wounded man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He exercised mercy, and did a neighbor’s part to one whom He did not account an alien.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this, then, He only replied that He had not a devil, but not that He was not a Samaritan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then after such an insult, this was all that He said of His own glory: “But I honor,” said He, “my Father, and ye dishonor me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is, I honor not myself, that ye may not think me arrogant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have One to honor; and did ye recognize me, just as I honor the Father, so would ye also honor me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do what I ought; ye do not what ye ought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And I,” said He, “seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom does He wish to be understood but the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, does He say in another place, “The Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son,” while here He says, “I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the Father judgeth, how is it that He judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In order to solve this point, attend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may be solved by [quoting] a similar mode of speaking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast it written, “God tempteth not any man;” and again thou hast it written, “The Lord your God tempteth you, to know whether you love Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just the point in dispute, you see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how does God tempt not any man, and how does the Lord your God tempt you, to know whether ye love Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is also written, “There is no fear in love but perfect love casteth out fear;” and in another place it is written, “The fear of the Lord is clean, enduring for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here also is the point in dispute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how does perfect love cast out fear, if the fear of the Lord, which is clean, endureth for ever?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are to understand, then, that there are two kinds of temptation: one, that deceives; the other, that proves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As regards that which deceives, God tempteth not any man; as regards that which proves, the Lord your God tempteth you, that He may know whether ye love Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But here again, also, there arises another question, how He tempteth that He may know, from whom, prior to the temptation, nothing can be hid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not that God is ignorant; but it is said, that He may know, that is, that He may make you to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such modes of speaking are found both in our ordinary conversation, and in writers of eloquence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let me say a word on our style of conversation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We speak of a blind ditch, not because it has lost its eyes, but because by lying hid it makes us blind to its existence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One speaks of “bitter lupins,” that is, “sour;” not that they themselves are bitter, but because they occasion bitterness to those who taste them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so there are also expressions of this sort in Scripture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who take the trouble to attain a knowledge of such points have no trouble in solving them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so “the Lord your God tempts you, that He may know.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “that He may know”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He may make you to know “if you love Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Job was unknown to himself, but he was not unknown to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He led the tempter into [Job], and brought him to a knowledge of himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then of the two fears?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a servile fear, and there is a clean [chaste] fear: there is the fear of suffering punishment, there is another fear of losing righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That fear of suffering punishment is slavish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What great thing is it to fear punishment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The vilest slave and the cruelest robber do so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is no great thing to fear punishment, but great it is to love righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has he, then, who loves righteousness no fear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly he has; not of incurring of punishment, but of losing righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, assure yourselves of it, and draw your inference from that which you love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some one of you is fond of money.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can I find any one, think you, who is not so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet from this very thing which he loves he may understand my meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is afraid of loss: why is he so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he loves money.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same measure that he loves money, is he afraid of losing it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, some one is found to be a lover of righteousness, who at heart is much more afraid of its loss, who dreads more being stripped of his righteousness, than thou of thy money.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the fear that is clean—this [the fear] that endureth for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not this that love makes away with, or casteth out, but rather embraces it, and keeps it with it, and possesses it as a companion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we come to the Lord that we may see Him face to face.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there it is this pure fear that preserves us; for such a fear as that does not disturb, but reassure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The adulterous woman fears the coming of her husband, and the chaste one fears her husband’s departure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as, according to one kind of temptation, “God tempteth not any man;” but according to another, “The Lord your God tempteth you;” and according to one kind of fear, “there is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear;” but according to another, “the fear of the Lord is clean, enduring for ever;”—so also, in this passage, according to one kind of judgment, “the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son;” and according to another, “I,” said He, “seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This point may also be solved from the word itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast penal judgment spoken of in the Gospel: “He that believeth not is judged already;” and in another place, “The hour is coming, when those who are in the graves shall hear His voice, and shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see how He has put judgment for condemnation and punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet if judgment were always to be taken for condemnation, should we ever have heard in the psalm, “Judge me, O God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the former place, judgment is used in the sense of inflicting pain; here, it is used in the sense of discernment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just because so expounded by him who says, “Judge me, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For read, and see what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this “Judge me, O God,” but just what he adds, “and discern my cause against an unholy nation”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because then it was said, “Judge me, O God, and discern [the true merits of] my cause against an unholy nation;” similarly now said the Lord Christ, “I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is there “one that seeketh and judgeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is the Father, who discerns and distinguishes between my glory and yours.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For ye glory in the spirit of this present world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so do I who say to the Father, “Father, glorify Thou me with that glory which I had with Thee before the world was.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “that glory”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One altogether different from human inflation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus doth the Father judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so to “judge” is to “discern.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what does He discern?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The glory of His Son from the glory of mere men; for to that end is it said, “God, Thy God, hath anointed Thee with the oil of gladness above Thy fellows.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not because He became man is He now to be compared with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, as men, are sinful, He is sinless; we, as men, inherit from Adam both death and delinquency, He received from the Virgin mortal flesh, but no iniquity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In fine, neither because we wish it are we born, nor as long as we wish it do we live, nor in the way that we wish it do we die: but He, before He was born, chose of whom He should be born; at His birth He brought about the adoration of the Magi; He grew as an infant, and showed Himself God by His miracles, and surpassed man in His weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lastly, He chose also the manner of His death, that is, to be hung on the cross, and to fasten the cross itself on the foreheads of believers, so that the Christian may say, “God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the very cross, when He pleased, He made His body be taken down, and departed; in the very sepulchre, as long as it pleased Him, He lay; and, when He pleased, He arose as from a bed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, brethren, in respect to His very form as a servant (for who can speak of that other form as it ought to be spoken of, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God”?)—in respect, I say, to His very form as a servant, the difference is great between the glory of Christ and the glory of other men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that glory He spoke, when the devil-possessed heard Him say, “I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what sayest Thou, O Lord, of Thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye say, “Thou hast a devil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I call you to life: keep my word and ye shall not die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They heard, “He shall never see death who keepeth my word,” and were angry, because already dead in that death from which they might have escaped.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said the Jews, Now we know that thou hast a devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never taste of death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how Scripture speaks: “He shall not see,” that is, “taste of death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He shall see death—he shall taste of death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who tasteth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What eyes has a man to see with when he dies?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When death at its coming shuts up those very eyes from seeing aught, how is it said, “he shall not see death”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With what palate, also, and with what jaws can death be tasted, that its savor may be discovered?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When it taketh every sense away, what will remain in the palate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But here, “he will see,” and “he will taste,” are used for that which is really the case, he will know by experience.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus spake the Lord (it is scarcely sufficient to say), as one dying to dying men; for “to the Lord also belong the issues from death,” as saith the psalm.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Seeing, then, He was both speaking to those destined to die, and speaking as one appointed to death Himself, what mean His words, “He who keepeth my saying shall never see death;” save that the Lord saw another death, from which He was come to deliver us—the second death, death eternal, the death of hell, the death of damnation with the devil and his angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is real death; for that other is only a removal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that other death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The leaving of the body—the laying down of a heavy burden; provided another burden be not carried away, to drag the man headlong to hell.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that real death then did the Lord say, “He who keepeth my saying shall never see death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not be frightened at that other death, but let us fear this one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, what is very grievous, many, through a perverse fear of that other, have fallen into this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It has been said to some, Adore idols; for if you do it not, you shall be put to death: or, as Nebuchadnezzar said, If you do not, you shall be thrown into the furnace of flaming fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many feared and adored.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shrinking from death, they died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Through fear of the death which cannot be escaped, they fell into that which they might happily have escaped, had they not, unhappily, been afraid of that which is inevitable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As a man, thou art born—art destined to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither wilt thou go to escape death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wilt thou do to escape it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That thy Lord might comfort thee in thy necessary subjection to death, of His own good pleasure He condescended to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou seest the Christ lying dead, art thou reluctant to die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Die then thou must; thou hast no means of escape.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it today, be it tomorrow; it is to be—the debt must be paid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, does a man gain by fearing, fleeing, hiding himself from discovery by his enemy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does he get exemption from death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, but that he may die a little later.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gets not security against his debt, but asks a respite.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Put it off as long as you please, the thing so delayed will come at last.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us fear that death which the three men feared when they said to the king, “God is able to deliver us even from that flame; and if not,” etc. There was there the fear of that death which the Lord now threatens, when they said, But also if He be not willing openly to deliver us, He can crown us with victory in secret.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence also the Lord, when on the eve of appointing martyrs and becoming the head-martyr Himself, said, “Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How “have they no more that they can do”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What if, after having slain one, they threw his body to be mangled by wild beasts, and torn to pieces by birds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cruelty seems still to have something it can do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to whom is it done?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has departed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The body is there, but without feeling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The tenement lies on the ground, the tenant is gone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so “after that they have no more that they can do;” for they can do nothing to that which is without sensation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But fear Him who hath power to destroy both body and soul, in hell fire.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here is the death that He spake of when He said, “He that keepeth my saying shall never see death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us keep then, brethren, His own word in faith, as those who are yet to attain to sight, when the liberty we receive has reached its fullness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those men, indignant, yet dead, and predestinated to death eternal, answered with insults, and said, “Now we know that thou hast a devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham is dead, and the prophets.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But not in that death which the Lord meant to be understood was either Abraham dead or the prophets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these were dead, and yet they live: those others were alive, and yet they had died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, replying in a certain place to the Sadducees, when they stirred the question of the resurrection, the Lord Himself speaks thus: “But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read how the Lord said to Moses from the bush, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, they live, let us labor so to live, that after death we may be able to live with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whom makest thou thyself,” they add, that thou sayest, “he shall never see death who keepeth my saying,” when thou knowest that both Abraham is dead and the prophets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing: it is my Father that glorifieth me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said this on account of their saying, “Whom makest thou thyself?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He refers His glory to the Father, of whom it is that He is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this expression also the Arians sometimes revile our faith, and say, See, the Father is greater; for at all events He glorifies the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Heretic, hast thou not read of the Son Himself also saying that He glorifies His Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If both He glorifieth the Son, and the Son glorifieth the Father, lay aside thy stubbornness, acknowledge the equality, correct thy perversity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is,” then, said He, “my Father that glorifieth me; of whom ye say, that He is your God: and ye have not known Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, my brethren, how He shows that God Himself is the Father of the Christ, who was announced also to the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say so for this reason, that now again there are certain heretics who say that the God revealed in the Old Testament is not the Father of Christ, but some prince or other, I know not what, of evil angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are Manicheans who say so; there are Marcionites who say so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are also, perhaps, other heretics, whom it is either unnecessary to mention, or all of whom I cannot at present recall; yet there have not been wanting those who said this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Attend, then, that you may have something also to affirm against such.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ the Lord calleth Him His Father whom they called their God, and did not know; for had they known [that God] Himself they would have received His Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But I,” said He, “know Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To those judging after the flesh He might have seemed from such words to be self-assuming, because He said, “I know Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But see what follows: “If I should say that I know Him not, I shall be a liar like unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not, then, self-assumption be so guarded against as to cause the relinquishment of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But I know Him, and keep His saying.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The saying of the Father He was speaking as Son; and He Himself was the Word of the Father, that was speaking to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day; and he saw, and was glad.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham’s seed, Abraham’s Creator, bears a great testimony to Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Abraham rejoiced,” He says, “to see my day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not fear, but “rejoiced to see it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in him there was the love that casteth out fear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says not, rejoiced because he saw; but “rejoiced that he might see.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believing, at all events, he rejoiced in hope to see with the understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he saw.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what more could the Lord Jesus Christ say, or what more ought He to have said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he saw,” He says, “and was glad.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can unfold this joy, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If those rejoiced whose bodily eyes were opened by the Lord, what joy was his who saw with the eyes of his soul the light ineffable, the abiding Word, the brilliance that dazzles the minds of the pious, the unfailing Wisdom, God abiding with the Father, and at some time come in the flesh and yet not to withdraw from the bosom of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this did Abraham see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in saying “my day,” it may be uncertain of what He spake; whether the day of the Lord in time, when He should come the flesh, or that day of the Lord which knows not a dawn, and knows no decline.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But for my part I doubt not that father Abraham knew it all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where shall I find it out?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ought the testimony of our Lord Jesus Christ to satisfy us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us suppose that we cannot find it out, for perhaps it is difficult to say in what sense it is clear that Abraham “rejoiced to see the day” of Christ, “and saw it, and was glad.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And though we find it not, can the Truth have lied?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us believe the Truth, and cherish no doubt of Abraham’s merited rewards.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet listen to one passage that occurs to me meanwhile.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When father Abraham sent his servant to seek a wife for his son Isaac, he bound him by this oath, to fulfill faithfully what he was commanded, and know also for himself what to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was a great matter that was in hand when marriage was sought for Abraham’s seed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that the servant might apprehend what Abraham knew, that it was not offspring after the flesh he desired, nor anything of a carnal kind concerning his race that was referred to, he said to the servant whom he sent, “Put thy hand under my thigh, and swear by the God of heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What connection has the God of heaven with Abraham’s thigh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already you understand the mystery: by thigh is meant race.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what was that swearing, but the signifying that of Abraham’s race would the God of heaven come in the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fools find fault with Abraham because he said, Put thy hand under my thigh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who find fault with Christ’s flesh find fault with Abraham’s conduct.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us, brethren, if we acknowledge the flesh of Christ as worthy of veneration, despise not that thigh, but receive it as spoken of prophetically.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For a prophet also was Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose prophet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of his own seed, and of his Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To his own seed he pointed in saying, “Put thy hand under my thigh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To his Lord he pointed in adding, “and swear by the God of heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The angry Jews replied, “Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was made, I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Weigh the words, and get a knowledge of the mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Before Abraham was made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, that “was made” refers to human formation; but “am” to the Divine essence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was made,” because Abraham was a creature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, Before Abraham was, I was; but, “Before Abraham was made,” who was not made save by me, “I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He say this, Before Abraham was made I was made; for “In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth;” and “in the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Before Abraham was made, I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recognize the Creator—distinguish the creature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who spake was made the seed of Abraham; and that Abraham might be made, He Himself was before Abraham.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, as if by the most open of all insults thrown at Abraham, they were now excited to greater bitterness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of a certainty it seemed to them that Christ the Lord had uttered blasphemy in saying, “Before Abraham was made, I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore took they up stones to cast at Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what could so great hardness have recourse, save to its like?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But Jesus” [acts] as man, as one in the form of a servant, as lowly, as about to suffer, about to die, about to redeem us with His blood; not as He who is—not as the Word in the beginning, and the Word with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when they took up stones to cast at Him, what great thing were it had they been instantly swallowed up in the gaping earth, and found the inhabitants of hell in place of stones?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It were not a great thing to God; but better was it that patience should be commended than power exerted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “He hid Himself” from them, that He might not be stoned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As man, He fled from the stones; but woe to those from whose stony hearts God has fled?
